page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.48xlarge is a storage-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture with Intel Xeon 8559C processors running at 3.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes a massive local storage capacity of 120,000 GB delivered via NVMe SSDs, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the i7ie.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. Multi-core CPU tests, such as stress-ng and Passmark CPU, place this instance in the top 10% of tested hardware. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth, particularly in large block uncached operations. Database operations and key-value store performance are highly competitive, with Redis SET operations reaching top-tier throughput. Single-core performance, however, sits in the middle tier, indicating that the instance is optimized for highly parallelized workloads rather than single-threaded execution speed.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7ie.48xlarge offers a dense consolidation of high-speed DDR5 memory and massive local NVMe storage on a dedicated hardware allocation. This resource density reduces the need for multiple smaller instances, optimizing licensing and management overhead for large-scale deployments. This instance is designed for storage-intensive and memory-heavy workloads, including large-scale relational databases, NoSQL databases, real-time data analytics, and high-throughput transactional systems.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.48xlarge is a storage-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors at 3.6 GHz. It is configured with 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and an extensive 120,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 100 Gbps baseline network. Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database processing capabilities, though single-core performance remains average. The high density of memory and local storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for massive datasets by minimizing network-attached storage dependencies. This instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, high-throughput transactional systems, and big data analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.48xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, 120,000 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps networking, optimized for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.48xlarge features 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 120,000 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe capacity and top-tier multi-core processing for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors
1536 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 120,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and big data analytics
